final:
// // This module implements a 4-to-1 multiplexer with selection lines determining the output based on a 2-bit selector. When the selector is '00', the output is assigned the value of input 'b'; when '01', the output takes the value of input 'a'; when '10', the output reflects the bitwise OR of 'a' and 'b'; and when '11', the output produces the bitwise AND of 'a' and 'b'.
module top_module(
  input [1:0] a,
  input [1:0] b,
  input [1:0] sel,
  output reg [1:0] out
);always @(*) begin
    if(sel == 2'b00) begin
      out = a;
    end
    else if(sel == 2'b01) begin
      out = b;
    end
    else if(sel == 2'b10) begin
      out = a & b;
    end
    else begin // sel == 2'b11
      out = a | b;
    end
  end

endmodule

When the selector is '00', the output is assigned the value of input 'b'; when '01', the output takes the value of input 'a'; when '10', the output reflects the bitwise OR of 'a' and 'b'; and when '11', the output produces the bitwise AND of 'a' and 'b'.
